title: campanopsis zeyheri
intro: Campanopsis zeyheri, also known as the Cape hound's tongue or Zeyher's hare's ear, is a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Campanopsis zeyheri has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from pain and discomfort. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or extract, making it accessible for herbal remedies and traditional medicine.
